identitySentence: Kerestine Law is a Denton solo practice where founding attorney Julia Kerestine has helped North Texas families with divorce, custody, mediation, and collaborative law since 1984.

concept.headline: A Denton Family Law Attorney Who Has Guided Families Since 1984
concept.subheadline: Divorce, custody, and support questions are hard enough without legal confusion on top. Julia Kerestine takes time to listen, explains your options in plain language, and helps you choose the path that fits your family.
credibilityPoints: Practicing law since 1984, more than 35 years in family law
Has mediated over 1,500 cases and holds the TMCA Distinguished Mediator credential (2010)
Trained in collaborative law and a member of Collaborative Divorce Denton County
Denton County Bar Association Director and CLE Director roles, with cases tried in all Denton County Courts

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: Kerestine Law handles the full range of family law matters, including divorce and uncontested divorce, child custody and visitation, child support, spousal support, property division, and post-divorce modifications. The firm also practices collaborative law and mediation, and assists with adoption, guardianship, paternity, pre-nuptial and post-nuptial agreements, and grandparent rights.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: From its office at 830 South Interstate 35 E in Denton, the firm serves families across Denton, Tarrant, Collin, Cooke, and Wise counties, and Julia Kerestine has tried cases in all Denton County Courts.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Your first consultation is a confidential conversation about your situation. Julia will ask about your family, your goals, and your concerns, then give you honest and realistic advice about your options, whether that is negotiation, mediation, collaborative law, or court.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Dear Kerestine Law,

Since 1984, Julia Kerestine has practiced family law in Denton, mediated more than 1,500 cases, and tried cases in all Denton County Courts. That depth of experience deserves an intake process that captures every inquiry it attracts.

Your site already invites visitors to schedule a consultation, and the contact form works. But like most firm sites, intake stops at a form or a phone call: there is no online scheduling, no automatic reply to the person reaching out at 9 pm about a custody question, and no way to know which inquiries were serious. For a solo family law practice where every consultation counts, that gap is where potential clients quietly move on to the next firm.

That is the one problem the Lead Capturer solves. It is a single, self-contained lead-capture page built specifically for Kerestine Law, with a custom identity sentence written for your firm:

"Kerestine Law is a Denton solo practice where founding attorney Julia Kerestine has helped North Texas families with divorce, custody, mediation, and collaborative law since 1984."

It includes a custom contact form built around your practice areas, from divorce and custody to mediation and collaborative law. Here is how it works once a potential client submits an inquiry:

- The inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox. Nothing sits in a dashboard you have to remember to check.
- The potential client automatically receives a topical response specific to their message, so a custody question gets a custody-focused reply.
- That response presents your available consultation times.
- A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the times on your calendar are held by people committed to showing up.

We would rather prove this than promise it. We are offering a 15-day proof period: we build the complete Lead Capturer for Kerestine Law, and you test the entire experience yourself before deciding anything.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):

1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter any confidential client information.
3. Confirm your firm's inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies your firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to the TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm the response addresses the actual topic of the inquiry.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter type.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will make it right or walk away. Fifteen days, one working demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.

Respectfully,

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
